players.name: Ramin REZAEIAN
players.highlights: Right back who dominated the flank — team-high 75 offers made, 9 crosses attempted, 21 of 30 line breaks completed, and scored the equaliser at 32'.

players.name: Mohammad MOHEBBI
players.highlights: Energetic goal-scorer — headed Iran's second equaliser at 64', 54 sprints (team high), 55 offers made and 25 in-behind runs to stretch the NZL defence.

players.name: Alireza BEIRANVAND
players.highlights: Kept Iran in contention with a 75% save percentage — made 6 saves from 8 shots on target including crucial stops from WOOD and McCOWATT.

players.name: Elijah JUST
players.highlights: Double goal-scorer (7' and 55'), highest physical output of any NZL starter with 161 high-speed runs and 70 sprints. Led NZL attacks on the left channel.

players.name: Chris WOOD
players.highlights: Constant aerial and physical menace — 4 shots on goal, won 5 aerial duels, and forced BEIRANVAND into two crucial saves before the final whistle.

players.name: Marko STAMENIC
players.highlights: Controlled the midfield battle — 7 tackles (5 won), 58 passes at 88% completion, 12 line breaks completed, and covered 10,496m across 92 minutes.

IRN.summary: Iran's 4-3-3 prioritised compact defending and rapid transitions. REZAEIAN operated as an attacking right back, generating width and volume on that flank. Despite only 43.3% possession, Iran created their best chances through counter-attacks and set pieces, with MOHEBBI's in-behind movement (25 such offers) being the primary attacking vehicle. Their 253 pressures and 46 forced turnovers disrupted NZL's build-up, though the defensive line conceded 8 shots on target.

NZL.summary: New Zealand's 4-4-2 controlled the match territorially with 56.7% possession and 89 line breaks completed — the most of any team in the group. JUST and McCOWATT provided constant wide threat while BELL and STAMENIC dominated the midfield engine room. Despite dominating large spells, New Zealand's xG of 1.47 from 8 shots on target reflected BEIRANVAND's quality in goal. Their high defensive line (defensive line height 48m when pressing) caught Iran offside repeatedly but also left space in behind.
